text: St Paul seems to assume that the qualifications needed by a bishop are those also needed by a presbyter; cf. I Tim. 3.1-7 and p. 218, note 4 and 6.

vv. 14-16: The false teachers in Crete are referred to in much the same terms as the false teachers at Ephesus (cf. I Tim. 1.4-11 and p. 217, note 1); in Crete, at any rate, they were Jews. They seem to have insisted particularly on the distinction between ‘clean’ and ‘unclean’ forms of food; thereby (as St Paul points out) denying the beneficent intentions of the Creator whom they professed to worship.
